title: 2026-27 FAFSA Federal Deadline
summary: June 30, 2027 is the federal deadline to submit the 2026-27 FAFSA. School and state priority deadlines may be earlier, so Green River students should still follow Green River's financial aid processing dates.

source.sourceLabel: Federal Student Aid FAFSA deadlines article

title: Green River College Foundation Scholarship
summary: Annual Green River College Foundation application for 300+ scholarships that can be used for tuition, fees, books, and required supplies at GRC. The 2026-2027 application closes April 30.

award.amountText: Average award is about $2,000; amount varies by scholarship

source.sourceLabel: Green River College Foundation scholarships apply page

title: Immigrants Rising Scholarship Database
summary: Database of scholarships and fellowships that do not require proof of U.S. citizenship, with filterable eligibility details and links to apply.

award.amountText: Varies

source.sourceLabel: Immigrants Rising scholarships and fellowships list

title: GRANCO Federal Credit Union Scholarship
summary: One-time GRANCO Federal Credit Union scholarship for graduating high school seniors or current students accepted into a college, community college, technical college, or other educational institution. The 2026 application window ran January 5 through March 27.

deadline.label: Application packet received or postmarked by March 27

source.sourceLabel: GRANCO Federal Credit Union 2026 scholarship page

title: UW Scholarships Database
organizationName: University of Washington Office of Admissions
summary: University of Washington scholarship information covering first-year, transfer, departmental, and financial aid scholarship pathways.

award.amountText: Varies

source.sourceLabel: UW Office of Admissions scholarships page

title: Silme Domingo & Gene Viernes Scholarship in Labor Studies
organizationName: Harry Bridges Center for Labor Studies
summary: University of Washington labor studies scholarship for graduate students and entering first-year or transfer students who show commitment to justice and equality, demonstrate financial need, and enroll for Autumn Quarter.

award.amountText: $6,000 total over two years

source.sourceLabel: Harry Bridges Center scholarship page

title: Washington Society of Professional Engineers Educational Foundation Engineering Scholarship Program
organizationName: Washington Society of Professional Engineers Educational Foundation
summary: One-time scholarship for Washington community college students transferring into ABET-accredited baccalaureate engineering programs at Washington universities and colleges.

source.sourceLabel: WSPE engineering scholarship page and application PDF

title: Green River Commencement Application Deadline 2026
summary: Submit the commencement application by this date. Green River notes that students must already have submitted an Intent to Graduate form to be added to the commencement registration system.

source.sourceLabel: Green River commencement for graduates page

title: Green River FAFSA/WASFA Priority Deadline for Spring 2027
organizationName: Green River College Financial Aid Office
summary: Complete your FAFSA or WASFA and submit all required Green River financial aid documents by this date to receive priority processing for Spring 2027. Meeting the deadline can help keep your classes from being dropped for non-payment while aid is being processed.

source.sourceLabel: Green River financial aid deadlines page

title: Green River FAFSA/WASFA Priority Deadline for Summer/Fall 2026
organizationName: Green River College Financial Aid Office
summary: Complete your FAFSA or WASFA and submit all required Green River financial aid documents by this date to receive priority processing for Summer/Fall 2026. Meeting the deadline can help keep your classes from being dropped for non-payment while aid is being processed.

source.sourceLabel: Green River financial aid deadlines page

title: Green River FAFSA/WASFA Priority Deadline for Winter 2027
organizationName: Green River College Financial Aid Office
summary: Complete your FAFSA or WASFA and submit all required Green River financial aid documents by this date to receive priority processing for Winter 2027. Meeting the deadline can help keep your classes from being dropped for non-payment while aid is being processed.

source.sourceLabel: Green River financial aid deadlines page

title: Green River Student Employment & Work Study
summary: On-campus student employment and work study openings for eligible Green River students. Good first step for building experience while enrolled.

source.sourceLabel: Green River student employment page

title: Green River Tech Internships
summary: Green River Devs internship board for technology students. Postings vary by company, location, hours, qualifications, and application details, so students should review each live listing before applying.

award.amountText: Pay varies by posting

source.sourceLabel: Green River Devs Internships page

title: WSOS Jobs Board
summary: Internship and job opportunity board shared through the Washington State Opportunity Scholarship career network. Postings vary by employer, role, location, deadline, and eligibility requirements.

award.amountText: Pay and benefits vary by posting

source.sourceLabel: WSOS Jobs Board

title: WSOS Career & Technical Scholarship
summary: Need-based scholarship for Washington residents in eligible STEM, health care, trades, and apprenticeship programs. The fall 2026 application window is closed; WSOS says the winter-spring application cycle opens September 29 and closes October 15, 2026 at 9 p.m.

award.amountText: Up to $1,500 per quarter for the duration of your program

source.sourceLabel: WSOS CTS applicant page

title: WWIN Star Scholars
summary: Scholarship for women living in Washington state who are pursuing a postsecondary credential up to a first bachelor's degree. Applicants must be 18 or older by September 1 of the award year, have a 2.0+ cumulative GPA, complete FAFSA or WASFA, and demonstrate unmet financial need. The 2026-27 cycle closed April 16, 2026; WWIN says the next cycle opens in January 2027.

award.amountText: Up to $5,000 per academic year; renewable up to $20,000

source.sourceLabel: WWIN Star Scholars page

title: Martin Achievement Scholarship
summary: Scholarship for Washington community college students planning ahead to transfer specifically to UW Seattle. Students apply over a year before transfer, with the 2026 cycle serving students planning to transfer in summer 2027, fall 2027, or winter 2028.

award.amountText: $5,000 for final community college year; $12,000/year up to 3 years at UW Seattle

source.sourceLabel: UW OMSFA Martin Achievement Scholarship page

title: Martin Family Foundation Honors Scholarship
summary: Scholarship for Washington community college students transferring to UW Seattle in the summer, fall, or winter immediately after the July 1 deadline. The application is open May 1 through July 1 each year, and students may apply before receiving their UW admission decision.

award.amountText: Up to $12,000 per year; renewable up to 3 years

source.sourceLabel: UW OMSFA Martin Honors Scholarship database page

title: Nelson-Mendenhall Summer Scholars Program
organizationName: University of Washington School of Pharmacy
summary: Funded 10-week summer research program for undergraduate students from two- and four-year colleges and universities to conduct hands-on pharmaceutical sciences research with UW School of Pharmacy faculty. The 2026 program runs June 22 through August 28 in Seattle.

award.amountText: $7,500 stipend

source.sourceLabel: UW School of Pharmacy Nelson-Mendenhall Summer Scholars page

title: NSF Research Experiences for Undergraduates (REU) Sites Directory
summary: Continuously updated directory of NSF-funded undergraduate research experiences across the United States and abroad. Students apply directly to individual REU Sites, which set their own deadlines and often provide stipends plus housing, meals, or travel support.

award.amountText: Stipends and support vary by REU site

source.sourceLabel: NSF Research Experiences for Undergraduates page

title: NSF ETAP Opportunity Search
summary: Search portal for NSF-funded education and training opportunities, including research experiences, summer programs, teaching assistantships, internships, career experience, scholarships, and fellowships. Deadlines, eligibility, and support vary by opportunity.

award.amountText: Stipends, scholarships, fellowships, and other support vary by opportunity

source.sourceLabel: NSF Education and Training Application search page

title: Smithsonian Research & Analysis Internship Opportunities
organizationName: Smithsonian Office of Academic Appointments and Internships
summary: Smithsonian internship listings for museum, research, science writing, and research and analysis roles across Smithsonian museums and research centers. Current 2026 listings include multiple summer opportunities with a February 15 deadline; students should check the live listings for the latest units and project openings.

award.amountText: Paid and unpaid options vary by Smithsonian opportunity

source.sourceLabel: Smithsonian Office of Academic Appointments and Internships opportunities page

title: WSOS Baccalaureate Scholarship
summary: Washington State Opportunity Scholarship pathway for Washington residents pursuing an eligible STEM or health care bachelor's degree at an eligible Washington college or university. Good fit for community college students planning to transfer or complete an eligible bachelor’s degree at a community college. The 2026 cycle closed February 26, 2026 at 9 p.m. PST.

award.amountText: Up to $22,500 total over up to 6 years

source.sourceLabel: Washington State Opportunity Scholarship page

title: Washington College Grant (WA Grant)
summary: Need-based Washington state grant for eligible Washington residents pursuing education beyond high school at an eligible college, program, or approved apprenticeship sponsor. Apply by completing the FAFSA or WASFA. Useful for community and technical college students because eligibility is tied to state residency, income, and eligible enrollment rather than a single school.

award.amountText: Varies by income, family size, and school/program cost; can cover full public tuition for eligible low-income students

source.sourceLabel: Washington Student Achievement Council page

title: BECU Foundation Scholarship
summary: Renewable scholarship for BECU members who are graduating high school seniors or current undergraduates enrolled in a credit-bearing accredited nonprofit technical, two-year, or four-year college or university. Applicants need a minimum 2.5 GPA through fall 2025 and must plan to attend full time in the academic year following the award.

award.amountText: $2,500 per year for up to 2 years; up to $5,000 total

source.sourceLabel: BECU Foundation page

title: WSECU Scholarship
summary: Annual scholarship program for WSECU members of all ages pursuing higher education, including two-year college, vocational school, four-year undergraduate, and graduate programs. WSECU states it awards over $100,000 each year.

award.amountText: $2,000 for two-year colleges and vocational schools; $3,000 for four-year undergraduate and graduate programs

source.sourceLabel: WSECU page

title: Seattle Foundation Scholarship Opportunities
summary: Regional scholarship portal hosted by Seattle Foundation and Scholarship America. Eligibility, deadlines, award amounts, and school limits vary by fund. Some scholarships support Washington students attending community colleges, technical schools, public universities, or other undergraduate programs.

award.amountText: Varies by scholarship

source.sourceLabel: Seattle Foundation Scholarship Opportunities page

title: Lambeth Family Scholarship
summary: Seattle Foundation scholarship for Washington high school seniors pursuing engineering, natural sciences, mathematics, computer science, or business. Eligible school pathways include public university, community college, or technical school.

award.amountText: $1,000-$3,000; renewable up to 4 years

source.sourceLabel: Seattle Foundation page

title: Pride Foundation Scholarships
summary: One application for 60+ scholarships supporting LGBTQ+ and questioning students across Alaska, Idaho, Montana, Oregon, and Washington. Eligible education pathways include community college, certificate, vocational, technical, trade, undergraduate, graduate, and professional programs. Pride Foundation does not check citizenship status when reviewing applications.

award.amountText: Varies by scholarship fund

source.sourceLabel: Pride Foundation page

title: Washington NASA Space Grant Scholarships
summary: Washington NASA Space Grant Consortium scholarship and research opportunity listing for students planning to study science, technology, engineering, or mathematics. UW's scholarship database lists opportunities for freshman, sophomore, junior, senior, and graduate students. Specific eligibility and deadlines vary by program.

deadline.label: February deadline; exact deadline varies by opportunity

award.amountText: Varies; UW undergraduate scholarship has offered up to $5,000

source.sourceLabel: Washington NASA Space Grant Consortium page

title: Seattle Colleges Foundation Scholarship
summary: One-application scholarship program for current and incoming students at North Seattle College, Seattle Central College, and South Seattle College. Part-time and full-time students, international students, DACA/undocumented students, BAS students, Seattle Promise students, and Running Start students are eligible and encouraged to apply.

award.amountText: Up to $5,000

source.sourceLabel: Seattle Colleges Foundation page

title: DOE Community College Internships (CCI)
organizationName: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Science / DOE National Laboratories
summary: Paid technical training internship for community college or accredited two-year college students interested in STEM careers. Students work onsite at one of the participating DOE laboratories, including possible Washington placement at PNNL depending on lab selection and availability. Applicants must be enrolled full-time or part-time at a community college/two-year college, have completed at least one semester, have at least 12 total credits and 6 STEM credits by the deadline, be 18+, and be a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident.

award.amountText: $650 weekly stipend; travel reimbursement and housing support may be available; typically 10 consecutive weeks, with some fall/spring flex-schedule options

source.sourceLabel: DOE Office of Science CCI pages

title: WA DOH Pathway Paid Summer Internships
summary: About 10-week paid summer internship for students enrolled at a Washington college and working toward an associate, undergraduate, or graduate degree. Students must be 18+ and not graduating before or during the internship. Work may be remote/virtual anywhere in Washington and matched to student experience, education, interests, and skills.

award.amountText: Around $20/hour; up to 35 hours/week for about 10 weeks

source.sourceLabel: WA Department of Health Pathway Paid Summer Internships page

title: DSHS Internship Program
organizationName: Washington State Department of Social and Health Services
summary: Washington DSHS offers multiple internship pathways, including unpaid credit-eligible internships, volunteer internships, MSW/BASW practicums, service member programs, and paid internships when available. Official DSHS pages currently show a conflict: the main internship page describes paid summer recruitment in January-March, while the unpaid internships page says the paid summer program is paused because of a hiring freeze. Use the unpaid/credit pathway as reliable and verify paid postings on careers.wa.gov each year.

award.amountText: Unpaid/credit eligible roles; paid opportunities may appear when funding/hiring status allows

source.sourceLabel: DSHS internship pages

title: Washington State Legislative Internship Program
summary: Legislative session internship in Olympia. Not a direct early-community-college option unless the student has junior/senior standing and can receive undergraduate academic credit toward a four-year degree. Students who transfer from community college often become eligible after reaching junior standing at a four-year school; BAS students at a community college may be eligible if they meet the credit and academic-credit requirements.

deadline.label: Applications open September 1, 2026 for the 2027 cohort

award.amountText: $2,400/month stipend for 2027 session; January 5-April 25, 2027

source.sourceLabel: Washington State Legislature Internship Program pages

title: Port of Seattle College and Graduate Internship Program
summary: Paid internships for undergraduate, graduate, recent graduate, and some technical certificate students in fields relevant to Port business objectives. Departments can include airport operations, engineering, environment and sustainability, IT, maritime operations, finance, communications, project management, and more.

deadline.label: Summer postings generally late February-early May

award.amountText: 100% paid internships; amount varies by posting

source.sourceLabel: Port of Seattle College and Graduate Internship Program page

title: Port of Tacoma Internships
summary: Paid internship opportunities across Port of Tacoma departments. Most positions are full-time during the summer, with occasional part-time academic-year roles. Applicants must be enrolled in an accredited school, college, or university and meet field-of-study qualifications for the specific role.

deadline.label: Posted each spring for summer employment

award.amountText: $20/hour for undergraduate students; $25/hour for postgraduate students

source.sourceLabel: Port of Tacoma Employment page

title: Washington State Internships
summary: Statewide internship information page for Washington agencies. Agencies may post internships through careers.wa.gov or market them individually, so students should use this as a starting point and check agency-specific postings.

award.amountText: Pay and eligibility vary by agency posting

source.sourceLabel: Washington State Internships page

title: WSDOT Internships
summary: Internship opportunities for college students or recent graduates in engineering, planning, communications, environmental work, and more. WSDOT also lists a wetlands ecology and monitoring techniques internship through The Evergreen State College for summer positions in Olympia.

award.amountText: Paid or unpaid depends on posting

source.sourceLabel: WSDOT Internships page

title: City of Seattle Internships
summary: Paid public-sector internships for students currently enrolled in an accredited certificate, two-year, four-year, or graduate program. Roles vary throughout the year and can include data and technology, urban planning, public health, community engagement, communications, HR, environmental science, and more.

award.amountText: Competitive hourly pay; benefits may include ORCA pass and paid sick/safe leave after 90 days

source.sourceLabel: City of Seattle Internships page

title: Seattle City Light College Internships
summary: Paid college internships with Seattle City Light in areas such as engineering, business, environmental studies, public administration, arts and sciences, communications, finance, accounting, HR, and environmental affairs. Students must be enrolled in accredited coursework leading to a graduate, baccalaureate, associate, or two-year certificate degree relevant to the internship.

award.amountText: Paid; summer roles typically full time for about 3 months; school-year roles usually part time for 3-12 months

source.sourceLabel: Seattle City Light College Internships page

title: Sound Transit Internships
summary: Paid internships in engineering, information technology, government and community outreach, business and labor compliance, planning and innovation, operations and public safety, and related fields. Applicants must be full-time or recently graduated students with a Washington State address.

award.amountText: Paid; full-time interns receive an ORCA card; exact pay varies by role

source.sourceLabel: Sound Transit Internships page

title: Washington NASA Space Grant Opportunities
summary: Washington NASA Space Grant opportunities page for STEM research, internship, fellowship, and student program listings. Eligibility, deadlines, awards, and host programs vary by opportunity, so students should check the live listing before applying.

award.amountText: Varies by opportunity

source.sourceLabel: Washington NASA Space Grant Opportunities page

title: UW Seattle Transfer Application Deadline for Winter Admission
summary: UW Seattle transfer applications for Winter admission open August 1 and are due September 1 each year.

source.sourceLabel: UW Office of Admissions transfer dates and deadlines

title: UW Seattle Transfer Application Deadline for Spring Admission
summary: UW Seattle transfer applications for Spring admission open November 1 and are due December 15 each year. Spring quarter is limited to applicants to specific departments in engineering and computer science.

source.sourceLabel: UW Office of Admissions transfer dates and deadlines

title: UW Seattle Transfer Application Deadline for Autumn/Summer Admission
summary: UW Seattle transfer applications for Autumn/Summer admission open December 15 and are due February 15 each year. Most degree-seeking transfer students apply for Autumn quarter; Summer admission has special notes on the UW page.

source.sourceLabel: UW Office of Admissions transfer dates and deadlines
